tables.name: MetadataAlignment
tables.label: Alignment Metadata
tables.desc: This lookup table records the list of allowable alignment values and names.

tables.name: MetadataCardinality
tables.label: Cardinality Metadata
tables.desc: This lookup table records the list of allowable cardinality values and names.

tables.name: MetadataColumnLookup
tables.label: Column Lookup Metadata
tables.desc: A record in this table corresponds to one of the lookups required for an end user to select a value for the corresponding column.  Selecting a value for a column may require multiple lookups down a table hierarchy.

tables.name: MetadataDatetimePrecision
tables.label: Datetime Precision Metadata
tables.desc: This lookup table records the list of allowable datetime precision values and names.

tables.name: MetadataDefaultType
tables.label: Default Type Metadata
tables.desc: This lookup table records the list of allowable default type values and names.

tables.name: MetadataDomainDetail
tables.label: Domain Detail Metadata
tables.desc: A record in this table corresponds to a member of a domain.

tables.name: MetadataDomainMaster
tables.label: Domain Master Metadata
tables.desc: A record in this table corresponds to a domain.  A domain is a fixed set of values to which a column may be restricted.  The same domain may serve as the domain for more than one column in a database.

tables.name: MetadataIndexDetail
tables.label: Index Detail Metadata
tables.desc: A record in this table corresponds to a column in an index for a table in a relational database.

tables.name: MetadataIndexMaster
tables.label: Index Master Metadata
tables.desc: A record in this table corresponds to an index for a table in a relational database.  The corresponding index may be either a unique index or a duplicate index.

tables.name: MetadataLogicalDataType
tables.label: Logical Data Type Metadata
tables.desc: This lookup table records the list of allowable logical data type values and names.

tables.name: MetadataPhysicalDataType
tables.label: Physical Data Type Metadata
tables.desc: This lookup table records the list of allowable physical data type values and names.

tables.name: MetadataRelationshipDetail
tables.label: Relationship Detail Metadata
tables.desc: A record in this table corresponds to one of the column pairs used to join two tables in a relational database.  A join between two tables may be based on one or more column pairs.

tables.name: MetadataRelationshipMaster
tables.label: Relationship Master Metadata
tables.desc: A record in this table corresponds to a relationship between two tables in a relational database.  There may be more than one relationship between the same two tables in a relational database.

tables.name: MetadataSortDirection
tables.label: Sort Direction Metadata
tables.desc: This lookup table records the list of allowable sort direction values and names.

tables.name: MetadataSortType
tables.label: Sort Type Metadata
tables.desc: This lookup table records the list of allowable sort type values and names.

tables.name: MetadataTable
tables.label: Table Metadata
tables.desc: A record in this table corresponds to a table in a relational database.

tables.name: MetadataTableCollection
tables.label: Table Collection Metadata
tables.desc: A record in this table corresponds to a collection of tables in a relational database that are managed as a group.  A table may be a member of only one table collection.

tables.name: MetadataTableColumn
tables.label: Table Column Metadata
tables.desc: A record in this table corresponds to a column in a table in a relational database.

tables.name: MetadataVersion
tables.label: Version Metadata
tables.desc: The table records the version of the data model of the corresponding database and the version of the data model of the corresponding metadata.  This table also records when the corresponding database was last altered and when the corresponding metadata was last loaded.

tables.name: chconsistence
tables.label: Horizon Consistence
tables.desc: The Horizon Consistence table contains descriptive terms of soil consistence -- rupture resistance, plasticity, and stickiness -- for the referenced horizon.  One row in this table is marked as having the representative characteristics for the horizon.

tables.name: chdesgnsuffix
tables.label: Horizon Designation Suffix
tables.desc: The Horizon Designation Suffix table contains the designation suffix(es), one per row,  for the referenced horizon.  For example, the "h" and "s" of a Bhs horizon appear as two rows in this table.

tables.name: chfrags
tables.label: Horizon Fragments
tables.desc: The Horizon Fragments table lists the mineral and organic fragments that generally occur in the referenced horizon.  If the Volume % is greater than zero (low=5, RV=10, high=15) in a row, the kind and size of fragment in that row exists everywhere this horizon and component occur in the map unit.  If the Volume % includes zero (low=0, RV=5, high=10), the kind and size of fragment may exist in some places, but not in others.

tables.name: chhuarts
tables.label: Horizon Human Artifacts
tables.desc: The Horizon Human Artifacts table lists the human created and/or modified objects included in the horizon shown in the Horizon table.  These are objects or materials created (or modified) by humans for practical purposes related to activities such as manufacturing, construction, or waste disposal. Examples of artifacts include processed wood products, liquid petroleum products, coal combustion by-products, asphalt, fibers and fabrics, bricks, cinder blocks, concrete, plastic, glass, rubber, pape

tables.name: chorizon
tables.label: Horizon
tables.desc:
The Horizon table lists the horizon(s) and related data for the referenced map unit component.  If the horizon thickness is greater than zero (low=5, RV=8, high=12), the horizon exists everywhere this component occurs.  If the horizon thickness includes zero (low=0, RV=1, high=3), the horizon may exist in some places, but not in other places.
Horizons that have two distinct parts, such as E/B or E&Bt horizons, are recorded twice.  Once for the characteristics of the first part; and again on ano

tables.name: chpores
tables.label: Horizon Pores
tables.desc: The Horizon Pores table lists the voids for the referenced horizon.  If the Quantity is greater than zero (low=2, RV=5, high=10) in a row, the voids in that row exist everywhere the horizon and component occur in the map unit.  If the Quantity includes zero (low=0, RV=2, high=5), the voids may exist in some places, but not in others.  More than one row can be marked as an RV row because a horizon may have more than one size or shape of void.

tables.name: chstruct
tables.label: Horizon Structure
tables.desc: The Horizon Structure table lists the individual soil structure size, grade, and shape terms for the referenced horizon.  Terms in this table are assembled into a structure group string which is recorded in the Horizon Structure Group table.

tables.name: chstructgrp
tables.label: Horizon Structure Group
tables.desc: The Horizon Structure Group table lists the ranges of  soil structure for the referenced horizon.  The row with the typically occurring structure is marked as being representative.  The entry in this table is based on grouping of entries in the Horizon Structure table.

tables.name: chtext
tables.label: Horizon Text
tables.desc: The Horizon Text table contains notes and narrative descriptions related to the referenced horizon.  Some notes may provide additional information about the horizon for which there is no explicit column for such data.  In many cases, the table is empty for a particular horizon.

tables.name: chtexture
tables.label: Horizon Texture
tables.desc: The Horizon Texture table lists the individual texture(s), or term(s) used in lieu of texture, for the referenced horizon.  Only the unmodified texture terms are listed in the Horizon Texture table; modifiers are listed in the Horizon Texture Modifier table.  For example, a gravelly loamy sand is shown as "GR-LS" in the Horizon Texture Group table, "ls" in the Horizon Texture table, and "gr" in the Horizon Texture Modifier table.

tables.name: chtexturegrp
tables.label: Horizon Texture Group
tables.desc: The Horizon Texture Group table lists the range of textures for the referenced horizon as a concatenation of horizon texture and texture modifier(s).  For example, a horizon that is gravelly loamy sand in some places and gravelly loamy coarse sand in other places is shown as GR-LS on one row and GR-LCOS on another row in this table.  The row with the typically occurring texture is identified as the RV row.  Stratified textures are shown in one row.  For example, a horizon that is stratified grav

tables.name: chtexturemod
tables.label: Horizon Texture Modifier
tables.desc: The Horizon Texture Modifier table lists the texture modifier(s) for the referenced texture.  For example, a gravelly loamy sand is shown as "GR-LS" in the Horizon Texture Group table, "ls" in the Horizon Texture table, and "gr" in this table.

tables.name: chunified
tables.label: Horizon Unified
tables.desc: The Horizon Unified table contains the Unified Soil Classification(s) for the referenced horizon.  One row in the Horizon Unified table is marked as the representative Unified classification for the horizon.

tables.name: clippolygon
tables.label: Clip Polygon
tables.desc: This table contains the feature class that is used for creating a subset of a soil survey area for that portion of that survey area that coincides with the specified geographic region.

tables.name: cocanopycover
tables.label: Component Canopy Cover
tables.desc: The Component Canopy Cover table lists the overstory plants that typically occur on the referenced map unit component.

tables.name: cocropyld
tables.label: Component Crop Yield
tables.desc: The Component Crop Yield table lists commonly grown crops and their expected range in yields when grown on the referenced map unit component.  Yields for the map unit as a whole are given in the Mapunit Crop Yield table.

tables.name: codiagfeatures
tables.label: Component Diagnostic Features
tables.desc: The Component Diagnostic Features table lists the typical soil features, such as ochric epipedon or cambic horizon, for the referenced map unit component.

tables.name: coecoclass
tables.label: Component Ecological Classification
tables.desc: The Component Ecological Classification table identifies the ecological sites typically associated with the referenced map unit component.  These may include the official NRCS forestland and rangland ecological sites, as well as those of other classification systems, such as the USFS Habitat Types.

tables.name: coeplants
tables.label: Component Existing Plants
tables.desc: The Component Existing Plants table lists the plants, either rangeland or forestland plants, that typically occur on the referenced map unit component.

tables.name: coerosionacc
tables.label: Component Erosion Accelerated
tables.desc: The Component Erosion Accelerated table lists the kinds of accelerated erosion that occur on the referenced map unit component.  One row in this table is marked as the representative kind of accelerated erosion for that component.

tables.name: coforprod
tables.label: Component Forest Productivity
tables.desc: The Component Forest Productivity table lists the site index and the annual productivity in cubic feet per acre per year (CAMI) of forest overstory tree species that typically occur on the referenced map unit component.

tables.name: coforprodo
tables.label: Component Forest Productivity - Other
tables.desc: The Component Forest Productivity - Other table lists the site index and annual productivity of forest overstory tree species in units other than cubic feet per acre per year for trees that typically occur on the referenced map unit component.

tables.name: cogeomordesc
tables.label: Component Geomorphic Description
tables.desc: The Component Geomorphic Description table lists the geomorphic features on which the referenced map unit component typically occurs.

tables.name: cohydriccriteria
tables.label: Component Hydric Criteria
tables.desc: The Component Hydric Criteria table lists the hydric soil criteria met for those referenced map unit components that are classified as a "hydric soil."

tables.name: cointerp
tables.label: Component Interpretation
tables.desc: The Component Interpretation table lists the predictions of behavior and limiting features for specified uses made for the referenced map unit component.

tables.name: coinundationtype
tables.label: Component Inundation Type
tables.desc: The Component Inundation Type table lists the type of inundation by water.

tables.name: comonth
tables.label: Component Month
tables.desc: The Component Month table lists the monthly flooding and ponding characteristics for the referenced map unit component.  This table has one row for each month of the year.

tables.name: component
tables.label: Component
tables.desc: The Component table lists the map unit components identified in the referenced map unit, and selected properties of each component.  If the Component % is greater than zero (low=65, RV=75, high=90) for a component, that component exists in every delineation of that mapunit.  If the Component % includes zero (low=0, RV=50, high=90), the component may exist in some delineations, but not in others.

tables.name: copm
tables.label: Component Parent Material
tables.desc: The Component Parent Material table lists the individual parent material(s) for the referenced map unit component.    In some cases where soils developed in multiple materials in a vertical sequence, that sequence will be noted.  In other cases multiple entries with no vertical sequence noted indicates the soil may have formed in one of the materials listed.

tables.name: copmgrp
tables.label: Component Parent Material Group
tables.desc: The Component Parent Material Group table lists the concatenated string of parent material(s) in which the referenced map unit component formed based on entries in the Component Parent Material table.  For example, a component formed in one parent material, such as loess, or one vertical sequence of parent materials, such as loamy glacial drift over silty residuum weathered from shale, has one row in this table.  A component formed in one parent material in some locations, but another parent mat

tables.name: copwindbreak
tables.label: Component Potential Windbreak
tables.desc: The Component Potential Windbreak table lists the windbreak plant species commonly recommended for the referenced map unit component.  A windbreak plant listed in this table may be used alone or in combination with other plants.

tables.name: corestrictions
tables.label: Component Restrictions
tables.desc: The Component Restrictions table lists the root restrictive feature(s) or layer(s) for the referenced map unit component.  If the thickness of the restrictive layer is greater than zero (low=5, RV=8, high=10), the restrictive layer exists in all delineations of the map unit where the component occurs.  If the thickness of the restrictive layer includes zero (low=0, RV=2, high=5), the restrictive layer may exist in some delineations, but not in others.  This table will be empty if the component d

tables.name: cosoilmoist
tables.label: Component Soil Moisture
tables.desc: The Component Soil Moisture table describes the typical soil moisture profile for the referenced map unit component during the month referenced in the Component Month table.  The soil moisture profiles for each month, taken as a group of twelve months, describe the representative situation for the component throughout the year.

tables.name: cosoiltemp
tables.label: Component Soil Temperature
tables.desc: The Component Soil Temperature table describes the typical soil temperature profile for the referenced map unit component during the month referenced in the Component Month table.  The soil temperature profiles for each month, taken as a group of twelve months, describe the representative situation for the component throughout the year.

tables.name: cosurffrags
tables.label: Component Surface Fragments
tables.desc: The Component Surface Fragments table lists the organic or mineral fragments that generally occur on the surface of the referenced map unit component.  If the cover percent is greater than zero (low=0.1, RV=1, high=3) for a row in this table, the fragment is in every delineation of the map unit where the referenced component occurs.  If the Cover % includes zero (low=0, RV=0.01, high=1) for a row in this table, the fragment may exist in some delineations and not in others.

tables.name: cosurfmorphgc
tables.label: Component Three Dimensional Surface Morphometry
tables.desc: The Component Three Dimensional Surface Morphometry table lists the typical geomorphic position (s) of the referenced map unit component, in three dimension terms.  The geomorphic position(s) listed in this table apply to the geomorphic feature referenced in the Component Geomorphic Description table.

tables.name: cosurfmorphhpp
tables.label: Component Two Dimensional Surface Morphometry
tables.desc: The Component Two Dimensional Surface Morphometry table lists the geomorphic position(s) of the referenced map unit component, in two dimensional hillslope profile terms.  The geomorphic position(s) listed in this table apply to the geomorphic feature referenced  in the Component Geomorphic Description table.

tables.name: cosurfmorphmr
tables.label: Component Microrelief Surface Morphometry
tables.desc: The Component Microrelief Surface Morphometry table lists microrelief features associated with the referenced geomorphic (microfeature) feature shown in the Component Geomorphic Description table.

tables.name: cosurfmorphss
tables.label: Component Slope Shape Surface Morphometry
tables.desc: The Component Slope Shape Surface Morphometry table lists the geomorphic shape(s) of the referenced map unit component, in slope shape terms.  The slope shape terms listed in this table apply to the referenced geomorphic feature shown in the Component Geomorphic Description table.

tables.name: cotaxfmmin
tables.label: Component Taxonomic Family Mineralogy
tables.desc: The Component Taxonomic Family Mineralogy table lists the mineralogy characteristics, as defined in Soil Taxonomy, that apply to the referenced map unit component.

tables.name: cotaxmoistcl
tables.label: Component Taxonomic Moisture Class
tables.desc: The Component Taxonomic Moisture Class table provides clear identification of the intended taxonomic moisture class, as defined in Soil Taxonomy, that apply to the referenced map unit component, even though moisture class is implied at a higher taxonomic level.  The class or classes listed in this table describe the representative situation for the component.

tables.name: cotext
tables.label: Component Text
tables.desc: The Component Text table contains notes and narrative descriptions for the referenced map unit component.  In many cases, the table will be empty for a particular component.

tables.name: cotreestomng
tables.label: Component Trees To Manage
tables.desc: The Component Trees To Manage table lists the trees commonly recommended for managing on the referenced map unit component.

tables.name: cotxfmother
tables.label: Component Taxonomic Family Other Criteria
tables.desc: The Component Taxonomic Family Other Criteria table lists the other taxonomic characteristics, such as classes of coatings or permanent cracks, as defined in Soil Taxonomy, that apply to the referenced map unit component.  The characteristics listed in this table describe the representative situation for the component.

tables.name: distinterpmd
tables.label: Distribution Interp Metadata
tables.desc: The Distribution Interp Metadata table records the set of NASIS fuzzy logic interpretations which were generated for the map unit components included in a set of distribution data.

tables.name: distlegendmd
tables.label: Distribution Legend Metadata
tables.desc: The Distribution Legend Metadata table records information about the legends or soil survey areas selected for inclusion in a set of distributed data.  The presence of a legend in this table does not imply that all of the available data for that legend was included in the set of data that was distributed.  Only certain map units and components for that legend may have been selected.  The record of the criteria used for selecting map units and components may be found in the Distribution Metadata

tables.name: distmd
tables.label: Distribution Metadata
tables.desc: The Distribution Metadata table records information associated with the selection of a set of data for distribution to some entity or information system external to NASIS.  A set of distribution data may include only selected map units from a legend or legends, and only selected components of those map units.  This table records the criteria used for selecting map units and components for inclusion in the set of distributed data.  Other recorded information includes the name of the NASIS user wh

tables.name: featdesc
tables.label: Feature Description
tables.desc: The Feature Description table records the description of all spot features that occur in a soil survey area.

tables.name: featline
tables.label: Feature Line
tables.desc: The Feature Line table records all spot feature symbols in a soil survey area that were digitized as one or more lines.

tables.name: featpoint
tables.label: Feature Point
tables.desc: The Feature Point table records all spot feature symbols in a soil survey area that were digitized as one or more points.

tables.name: gsmmupolygon
tables.label: General Soil Map Mapunit Polygon
tables.desc: This table contains the map unit polygon feature classes for the general soil map of the United States and some of its territories.  This coverage is historically known as "STATSGO".  In addition to the map unit feature class for the entire US, this table includes the individual state map unit feature classes.

tables.name: laoverlap
tables.label: Legend Area Overlap
tables.desc: The Legend Area Overlap table lists the geographic areas that are coincident with the soil survey area identified in the Legend table.  For example, a survey area that covers two counties would have two rows in this table, one for each county.  Other types of geographic areas listed might include state, MLRA, rainfall (R) factor area, climate (C) factor area, etc.

tables.name: legend
tables.label: Legend
tables.desc: The Legend table identifies the soil survey area that the legend is related to, and related information about that legend.

tables.name: legendtext
tables.label: Legend Text
tables.desc: The Legend Text table contains notes and narrative descriptions related to the referenced legend.  Legend text is optional.  In many cases, this table is empty.

tables.name: mapunit
tables.label: Mapunit
tables.desc: The Mapunit table identifies the map units included in the referenced legend.  Data related the map unit as a whole are also given.

tables.name: muaggatt
tables.label: Mapunit Aggregated Attribute
tables.desc: The Mapunit Aggregated Attribute table records a variety of soil attributes and interpretations that have been aggregated from the component level to a single value at the map unit level. They have been aggregated by one or more appropriate means in order to express a consolidated value or interpretation for the map unit as a whole.

tables.name: muaoverlap
tables.label: Mapunit Area Overlap
tables.desc: The Mapunit Area Overlap table lists the map units that exist in the overlap between the entire soil survey area and the referenced geographic area in the Legend Area Overlap table.

tables.name: mucropyld
tables.label: Mapunit Crop Yield
tables.desc: The Mapunit Crop Yield table lists commonly grown crops and their expected yields for the referenced map unit as a whole.  Yields for individual map unit components are given in the Component Crop Yield table.

tables.name: muline
tables.label: Mapunit Line
tables.desc: The Mapunit Line table records all map units in a soil survey area that were digitized as one or more lines.

tables.name: mupoint
tables.label: Mapunit Point
tables.desc: The Mapunit Point table records all map units in a soil survey area that were digitized as one or more points.

tables.name: mupolygon
tables.label: Mapunit Polygon
tables.desc: The Mapunit Polygon table records all map units in a soil survey area that were digitized as one or more polygons.

tables.name: mutext
tables.label: Mapunit Text
tables.desc: The Mapunit Text table contains notes and narrative descriptions related to the referenced map unit.

tables.name: numbers
tables.label: Numbers
tables.desc: Table created for the sole purpose to help WSS performance.

tables.name: sacatalog
tables.label: Survey Area Catalog
tables.desc: The Survey Area Catalog table catalogs all soil survey areas that exist in the data mart.  A survey area cannot exist in the data mart unless at least minimal spatial data (survey area boundary with no corresponding map unit polygons) exists.  A survey area cannot be selected for downloading unless tabular data also exists.

tables.name: sainterp
tables.label: Survey Area Interpretion
tables.desc: The Survey Area Interpretation table records information about the soil interpretations that were generated for a survey area.

tables.name: sapolygon
tables.label: Survey Area Polygon
tables.desc: The Survey Area Polygon table records the polygon or polygons that represent a soil survey area boundary.

tables.name: saspatialver
tables.label: Survey Area Spatial Version
tables.desc: The Survey Area Spatial Version table records information about the version of underlying spatial data.  This table also serves as the parent table of all spatial table hierarchies, which due to corresponding cascade delete rules, allows all spatial data for a survey area to be deleted by simply deleting the appropriate record in this table.

tables.name: sastatusmap
tables.label: Survey Area Status Map
tables.desc: This table records the polgons that make up the survey area status map.  This map may include survey areas for which corresponding tabular data is not yet available.

tables.name: satabularver
tables.label: Survey Area Tabular Version
tables.desc: The Survey Area Tabular Version table records information about the version of underlying tabular data.  This table also serves as the parent table of all tabular table hierarchies, which due to corresponding cascade delete rules, allows all tabular data for a survey area to be deleted by simply deleting the appropriate record in this table.

survey-area.label: Survey Area
survey-area.desc: Survey area and map legend tables

map-unit.label: Map Unit
map-unit.desc: Map unit attributes and spatial aggregates

spatial.label: Spatial
spatial.desc: Polygon, line, and point geometry tables
